title: Create a Movie Review App with URL Mapping

source.module: Core Django fundamentals - Views and URLs

topicTags: Core Django fundamentals - Views and URLs

content.problemStatement:
In this worked out example, we'll explain the functionality of `include()`. \
Our project structure is as follows
```python
mymovies/
├── manage.py
├── mymovies/            ← Project
│   └── urls.py          ← Main URL config
├── reviews/             ← App
│   ├── views.py
│   └── urls.py          ← App-specific URL config
```

### How does this code work?

1. When someone visits the home page - `/` - they see the following 'Welcome to the Movie Reviews App'
2. When you type `/reviews/`, Django first looks into `mymovies/urls.py` and then in `reviews/urls.py` to continue matching the rest of the URL.
   - `/reviews/` → goes to views.home
   - `/reviews/inception/` → goes to views.inception_review and shows reviews about this movie from `views.py` by calling the function `def inception_review(request):`
   - `/reviews/interstellar/` → goes to views.interstellar_review
   - `/reviews/thedarkknight/` → goes to views.the_dark_knight_review

Click on Run to see the code in action. \
Change the URL to `/`, `/reviews`, `/reviews/interstellar/` to see the different webpages.
